title: Daphne kulingensis
taxonomy: Daphne kulingensis is classified as a species within the genus Daphne. The genus Daphne is part of the family Thymelaeaceae, which consists of shrubs and small trees. intro: Daphne kulingensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, native to the mountainous regions of southwestern China. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Daphne kulingensis include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and diterpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. Daphne kulingensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusions in traditional Chinese medicine, as well as being used to make herbal teas and extracts for the treatment of various health conditions.
